message:
fix(wills-estate): further soften attorney-handoff claims per linter review

- "attorney-ready handoff package" → "handoff packet for attorney review"
- "packet already done" → "packet already organized"
- "dramatically shorter" → "more efficient"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix any contrast issues, particularly in the slate/gray color palette.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T02:27:38.786060629Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T03:29:08.805754935Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T03:29:08.805754935Z","close_reason":"Fixed WCAG AA contrast issues: text-slate-600→text-slate-500, kbd elements text-slate-400/500→text-slate-300 for proper contrast on bg-slate-800","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cfb.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cfb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T02:27:38.786976460Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cfb.6","title":"Comprehensive reduced motion support","description":"Audit all animations and ensure they respect prefers-reduced-motion. Provide alternative static states or simplified animations for users who prefer reduced motion.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T02:27:38.816703165Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T04:04:45.339141998Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T04:04:45.339141998Z","close_reason":"Added useReducedMotion to all framer-motion components: hero.tsx, command-palette.tsx, section-shell.tsx, scroll-to-top.tsx, keyboard-shortcuts-modal.tsx, bottom-sheet.tsx, client-shell.tsx, site-header.tsx. CSS animations already had prefers-reduced-motion media query. Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cfb.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cfb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T02:27:38.817504821Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ch3","title":"Add embedded podcast players to Nvidia page","description":"# Task: Embedded Podcast Players ## Objective Allow visitors to listen to podcast episodes about the Nvidia essay without leaving the page. ## Episodes to Embed ### 1. Bankless Podcast (Primary) - Title: "DeepSeek R1 & The Short Case For Nvidia Stock" - Link: https://www.bankless.com/podcast/deepseek-r1-the-short-case-for-nvidia-stock-jeffrey-emanuel - Platform: Bankless (likely has embed option) - Date: January 28, 2025 ### 2. Delphi Digital - Title: "Viral Author of The Short Case for Nvidia Stock" - Link: https://members.delphidigital.io/media/jeffrey-emanuel-viral-author-of-the-short-case-for-nvidia-stock-cohosted-by-pondering-durian - May be members-only (link instead of embed?) ### 3. Farzad Podcast - Title: "Viral NVIDIA Short on Record Breaking $600B Loss" - Link: https://open.spotify.com/episode/59UKRipYaHjsrQjEXHFR5z - Platform: Spotify (has embed) ## Implementation Options ### Option A: Native Embeds - Use each platform's embed iframe - Pros: Native player experience, no extra dependencies - Cons: Different visual styles, potential loading issues ### Option B: Just Links with Preview Cards - Rich link cards with episode title, duration, platform logo - Pros: Consistent styling, faster load, no third-party tracking - Cons: Users leave the page to listen ### Option C: Hybrid - One featured episode with embed (Bankless - most comprehensive) - Others as link cards ## Recommended: Option C (Hybrid) - Bankless episode gets Spotify/native embed - Others get styled link cards with "Listen on Spotify" / "Listen on Delphi" buttons - Lazy load embeds to avoid performance hit ## Accessibility - Embeds should have proper aria-labels - Alternative text links for those who can't use embeds ## Dependencies - Depends on: Layout design task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:03:44.870815517Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T02:41:49.741390711Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T02:41:49.741390711Z","close_reason":"Created NvidiaPodcastSection component with lazy-loaded Spotify embeds, podcast cards with hover effects, featured/regular layouts. Component respects reduced motion and has loading/error states for embeds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ch3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-azv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:04:16.210090875Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cm1s","title":"AxiomCoherenceViz emits SVG circle radius console errors on article load","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:32:13.767147230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:32:18.993894567Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cm1s","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:32:13.767147230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cm1s","title":"AxiomCoherenceViz emits SVG circle radius console errors on article load","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:32:13.767147230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:36:02.990173186Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:36:02.986666397Z","close_reason":"Resolved by commit aba5fb3, which removes the brittle animated SVG circle radius in AxiomCoherenceViz and replaces it with a static circle so the article no longer logs circle radius console errors on load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cm1s","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:32:13.767147230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cujd","title":"Evaluate WASM candidates for CPU-intensive operations","description":"# WASM Candidacy Evaluation ## Part of Feature Performance Optimization Initiative ## Objective Identify CPU-intensive operations that could benefit from Rust-to-WASM compilation for improved performance. ## Background WebAssembly (WASM) can provide significant performance gains for: - Heavy mathematical computations - Data processing loops - Cryptographic operations - Image/video processing However, WASM has overhead: - ~10-100μs for JS↔WASM boundary crossing - Additional bundle size (~100KB+ for Rust runtime) - Increased build complexity ## Analysis Methodology ### 1. Profile CPU Usage Examined all components for CPU-intensive patterns: - Loops processing large datasets - Complex mathematical calculations - Recursive algorithms - Real-time data processing ### 2. Candidates Evaluated #### stats-grid.tsx: parseStatValue() ```tsx function parseStatValue(value: string): { number: number; suffix: string; isAnimatable: boolean } { const match = value.match(/^([0-9,.]+)(K|M|B)?(\+)?$/i); // ... simple regex and string manipulation } ``` **Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM** - Execution time: <1μs per call - Called: ~4 times total (for 4 stats) - Total time: <4μs - WASM overhead would exceed computation time #### tldr-tool-grid.tsx: Fuse.js Search ```tsx const fuse = new Fuse(tools, { ... }); const results = fuse.search(query); ``` **Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM** - Already optimized by Fuse.js library - Dataset: ~15 tools (small) - Execution time: ~1-2ms - WASM boundary crossing would negate benefits #### three-scene.tsx: Star Generation ```tsx for (let i = 0; i < numPoints; i++) { const r = 5 + rand() * 4; const theta = rand() * Math.PI * 2; // ... trigonometry } ``` **Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM** - Runs once during initialization - numPoints: 100-400 depending on quality - Execution time: <5ms - Result is cached in useMemo #### AnimatedNumber: Counter Animation ```tsx const easeOutExpo = (t: number) => (t === 1 ? 1 : 1 - Math.pow(2, -10 * t)); setCount(easedProgress * end); ``` **Verdict: ❌ Not suitable for WASM** - Simple mathematical expression - Called via requestAnimationFrame - Execution time: <100μs per frame - JS is more than sufficient ### 3. Conclusion **No operations in this codebase warrant WASM optimization.** Reasons: 1. **Scale too small**: Operations process 4-400 items, not millions 2. **Overhead exceeds benefit**: WASM boundary crossing costs more than the computation 3. **Already optimized**: Heavy operations (Three.js rendering) are already GPU-accelerated 4. **Complexity cost**: WASM adds build toolchain, maintenance burden ### 4. When WASM WOULD Be Appropriate For reference, WASM makes sense for: - Image processing (thousands of pixels) - Real-time audio processing - Physics simulations with many objects - Cryptographic operations - Data compression/decompression ## Recommendation Focus optimization efforts on React rendering patterns (useMemo, React.memo) rather than WASM. The performance bottlenecks in this site are render cycles, not computation. ## Future Consideration If the site ever includes: - Client-side markdown rendering of very long documents - Complex data visualizations with thousands of points - Real-time collaborative editing Then WASM should be re-evaluated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:50:25.783888310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:34.624118446Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:34.624118446Z","close_reason":"No WASM candidates found. parseStatValue (<1μs), Fuse.js search (~2ms), star generation (~5ms) - all too fast for WASM overhead to provide benefit. Focus shifted to React rendering optimizations.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cujd","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-7ej3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:52:57.468589007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-cw4","title":"Add cass_memory_system to content.ts (152 stars)","description":"## What Add cass_memory_system project to the projects array in jeffreyemanuel-site/lib/content.ts ## Project Details - **Name**: cass_memory_system (or "CASS Memory System") - **GitHub**: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system - **Stars**: 152 - **Description**: No GitHub description currently - need to research ## Background & Context - CASS = Coding Agent Session Search (likely related to coding_agent_session_search) - This appears to be a memory/persistence layer for the CASS ecosystem - Part of Jeffrey's Flywheel tooling for AI coding agents - Research code at /data/projects/cass_memory_system/ to understand actual purpose ## Implementation Research the codebase first, then add: ```typescript { name: "CASS Memory System", kind: "oss", badge: "152 stars", href: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system", short: "", description: "", tags: ["Memory", "Agents", "Search", ""], } ``` ## Research Questions - What does CASS stand for in this context? - How does it relate to coding_agent_session_search? - What memory/persistence capabilities does it provide? - What language is it written in? ## Acceptance Criteria - Project appears in projects array - Description accurately reflects actual functionality - Relationship to other CASS tools is clear if relevant","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:23:51.537101434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:11:52.232950422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:11:52.232950422Z","close_reason":"CASS Memory System already exists - updated star count from 60 to 152","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-d20","title":"Bug: Star count formatting inconsistent (should use K/M format)","description":"# Star Count Formatting Inconsistency ## The Problem Tool cards display star counts using `toLocaleString()` which produces "1,400" but the site-wide pattern (used in hero stats, other pages) uses K/M format like "1.4K" or "3.6K+". ## Current Behavior - `tldr-tool-card.tsx:237`: `{tool.stars.toLocaleString()}` → "1,400" - Hero stats show "3,600+" (hardcoded string, not calculated) - Creates visual inconsistency and makes numbers harder to scan ## Expected Behavior - Star counts should display as "1.4K", "891", "132" etc. - Numbers under 1000 show as-is - Numbers ≥1000 show with K suffix (one decimal if needed) - Numbers ≥1000000 show with M suffix ## Files to Modify - `components/tldr-tool-card.tsx` - Line 237, star display - Consider creating shared util in `lib/utils.ts` for `formatStarCount()` ## Implementation Notes - Site already has `formatNumber()` in `lib/github-stats.ts` - consider reusing or unifying - Pattern: `num >= 1000 ? (num / 1000).toFixed(1).replace(/\.0$/, '') + 'K' : num.toString()` - Ensure screen readers get full number via aria-label ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Star counts display in K/M format consistently - [ ] Numbers under 1000 display as-is (no decimal) - [ ] Aria-label contains full number for accessibility - [ ] Visual consistency with rest of site","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:50:06.691901045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T00:26:05.223756529Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T00:26:05.223756529Z","close_reason":"Fixed star count formatting in tldr-tool-card.tsx to use formatStarCount() for K/M format, added accessibility labels with full numbers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-d20","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ktq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-13T00:18:30.845617847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} @@ -247,6 +247,7 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-nil","title":"Research Coding Agent Session Search (CASS) source code","description":"# Task: Research Coding Agent Session Search (CASS) Source Code ## Purpose Review the CASS source code for accurate /tldr page content. ## Source Location - Path: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search - Language: Rust - GitHub: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search ## Research Questions ### Functionality 1. What agent session formats are supported? (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini) 2. How does full-text search work? 3. Robot mode (--robot) output format? 4. Search query syntax? 5. Result ranking/relevance? ### Implementation 1. Tantivy search engine integration? 2. Index storage and updates? 3. Performance characteristics? 4. Cross-platform support? ### Integrations 1. How does CM use CASS for memory retrieval? 2. How does NTM expose search across agent histories? 3. How does BV link search results to tasks? ## Deliverable Structured summary emphasizing unified search across multiple agent types and the Rust/Tantivy performance benefits.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:17:35.000442511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:48.581550034Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:48.581482649Z","close_reason":"Content for this tool already exists in lib/content.ts with comprehensive whatItDoes, whyItsUseful, implementationHighlights, synergies, techStack, keyFeatures, and useCases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-nn9","title":"Bug: Missing error boundary on tool grid (crash risk)","description":"# Missing Error Boundary on Tool Grid ## The Problem The TldrToolGrid component has no error boundary. If any tool card throws an error (e.g., missing data, invalid icon), the entire page crashes with a white screen. ## Risk Assessment - **Likelihood**: Medium - data comes from static content.ts but icon mapping could fail - **Impact**: High - entire page becomes unusable - **Current behavior**: React's default "white screen of death" ## Current Architecture ``` TldrPage ├── TldrHero (no error boundary) ├── FlywheelExplanation │ └── TldrSynergyDiagram (no error boundary) └── TldrToolGrid (no error boundary) ← HERE └── TldrToolCard × N (any could throw) ``` ## Expected Behavior - If one tool card fails, show graceful error UI for that card only - Rest of page continues working - Error is logged for debugging - User can still interact with other tools ## Implementation Options ### Option A: Wrap each card (Recommended) ```tsx {coreTools.map((tool) => ( }> ))} ``` ### Option B: Wrap entire grid - Simpler but loses granularity - One error takes down all cards ## Files to Modify - `components/tldr-tool-grid.tsx` - Wrap card rendering - May need to create `components/tool-card-error.tsx` for fallback UI ## Error Fallback Design - Show tool name and "Error loading details" - Include "Report issue" link to GitHub - Match card dimensions to prevent layout shift ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Error boundary wraps tool cards - [ ] Graceful fallback UI when card fails - [ ] Error logged to console with tool ID - [ ] Other cards continue working - [ ] No layout shift from error state","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:50:24.663243438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T00:29:22.009637621Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T00:29:22.009637621Z","close_reason":"Added ErrorBoundary components wrapping: 1) entire page content, 2) FlywheelExplanation section, 3) TldrToolGrid section. Provides graceful degradation if any section crashes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-nn9","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r0i","type":"child","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:56:29.119410793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-o3v","title":"Build Try It section grid/showcase component","description":"## Overview Create the section component that displays all demo cards in an attractive, browsable format. ## Layout Options 1. **Featured + Grid**: One large featured, others in grid below 2. **Category Tabs**: Grouped by category with tab navigation 3. **Horizontal Carousel**: Swipeable on mobile Recommend: Featured + Grid for simplicity and impact. ## Technical Implementation - Map over demos data to render cards - Optional filtering by category - Responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 tablet, 3 desktop) - Staggered reveal animation on scroll ## Section Structure ```tsx

Try It Yourself

Explore live demos of my projects

{demos.map(demo => )}
``` ## Animations - Staggered fade-in on scroll into view - Individual card hover effects - Reduced motion fallback ## Empty State - Handle case where no demos available (shouldn't happen but defensive) ## Dependencies - Demo card component must be complete - Demo content data must be ready ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Grid displays all demos attractively - [ ] Responsive at all viewports - [ ] Animations smooth - [ ] Accessible","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:11:25.647855025Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:55:57.372419557Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T21:55:57.372419557Z","close_reason":"Created DemoShowcase component (components/demo-showcase.tsx) with responsive grid layout, optional category filtering, staggered Framer Motion animations, empty state handling, and configurable props. Uses DemoCard component and liveDemos data. All static analysis passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-o3v","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-do1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:12:16.909554369Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-o3v","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-rle","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:12:16.943710053Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-oca1","title":"Wills article Playwright webServer crashes bun dev with Turbopack task DB corruption","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:36:02.713576049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:36:02.713576049Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-oca1","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:36:02.713576049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ocbu","title":"Fix Unit Test Environment and Compatibility","description":"Resolve 'document is not defined' errors in component tests by correctly configuring the JSDOM environment for Bun/Vitest. Fix 'vi.mocked', 'vi.importActual', and 'vi.resetModules' compatibility issues.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:04:38.720178468Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T18:08:50.163584451Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T18:08:50.163584451Z","close_reason":"Resolved unit test environment issues by ensuring 'bun run test' (Vitest) is used instead of 'bun test' (native Bun runner). Verified that JSDOM environment is correctly loaded and 'document' is available. Fixed 'vi' import in 'tldr-test-utils.test.ts'. All 191 unit tests passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-ocbu","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-pvmw","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:04:38.800434829Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-okh","title":"Feature: Spring physics for card expand/collapse","description":"# Spring Physics for Card Expand/Collapse ## Overview Replace the current basic height animation with spring-based physics for a more natural, premium feel when expanding and collapsing tool cards. ## Current State - `tldr-tool-card.tsx` lines 285-290 - Uses `{ height: 'auto', opacity: 1 }` with basic `duration: 0.3` - Feels mechanical and lacks life - Content appears all at once ## Proposed Animation ### Spring Parameters ```tsx // Natural, bouncy feel without being cartoonish const springConfig = { type: 'spring', stiffness: 300, // Quick but not snappy damping: 25, // Some bounce but settles fast mass: 0.8, // Lightweight feel }; ``` ### Expand Choreography ``` T+0ms: Container height begins expanding (spring) T+50ms: "Why It's Useful" section fades in T+100ms: "Key Features" section fades in T+150ms: "Implementation Highlights" fades in T+200ms: "Tech Stack" fades in T+250ms: "Synergies" section fades in T+300ms: "Use Cases" fades in ``` ### Collapse (Reverse) - Content fades out all at once (faster) - Then height collapses with spring ## Technical Implementation ### Container Animation ```tsx ``` ### Staggered Content ```tsx const contentVariants = { hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 10 }, visible: (i: number) => ({ opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { delay: i * 0.05, duration: 0.3, } }), }; // In render {sections.map((section, i) => ( {section.content} ))} ``` ### Using AnimatePresence Properly ```tsx {isExpanded && ( {/* Content with stagger */} )} ``` ## Files to Modify - `components/tldr-tool-card.tsx` - Lines 283-394 ## Before/After Comparison | Aspect | Before | After | |--------|--------|-------| | Height change | Linear 0.3s | Spring (bouncy settle) | | Content reveal | All at once | Staggered sections | | Collapse | Same as expand | Faster, content first | | Feel | Mechanical | Natural, premium | ## Reduced Motion - Instant expand/collapse (no animation) - Content still staggers but instantly (opacity only) - No spring bounce ## Performance Notes - `layout` prop can cause expensive recalculations - Test with multiple cards expanded - Use `layoutId` only if necessary ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Expand uses spring physics for height - [ ] Content sections stagger in on expand - [ ] Collapse is faster than expand - [ ] Animation feels natural, not cartoonish - [ ] No layout shift or jank - [ ] Works with multiple cards expanded - [ ] Respects reduced motion (instant) - [ ] Maintains proper focus management","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:54:06.662012321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:10:44.492453887Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:10:44.492386972Z","close_reason":"N/A - current design uses bottom sheet on mobile and full content on desktop; no in-card expand/collapse exists","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-okh","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r0i","type":"child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-or1x","title":"Memoize stats-grid.tsx parseStatValue results","description":"# Memoize Stats Grid Parsing ## Part of Feature React Rendering Optimization ## Problem Statement In `components/stats-grid.tsx`, the `parseStatValue` function was being called inside the render loop for each stat on every render cycle. ### Before (Problematic Pattern) ```tsx {stats.map((stat, index) => { const parsed = parseStatValue(stat.value); // Called every render! return ( ); })} ``` ### Why This Matters 1. **Redundant computation**: `parseStatValue` performs regex matching 2. **Re-renders frequent**: Parent components can trigger re-renders 3. **Accumulates**: Even microseconds add up with React's reconciliation ## Solution Implemented ### Added useMemo Import ```tsx import { useEffect, useRef, useState, useMemo } from "react"; ``` ### Memoized All Parsed Stats Together ```tsx // Memoize parsed stat values to avoid re-parsing on each render const parsedStats = useMemo( () => stats.map((stat) => ({ stat, parsed: parseStatValue(stat.value) })), [stats] ); ``` ### Updated Render Loop ```tsx {parsedStats.map(({ stat, parsed }, index) => { // ... uses pre-computed parsed value })} ``` ## Technical Details ### parseStatValue Function ```tsx function parseStatValue(value: string): { number: number; suffix: string; isAnimatable: boolean; } { const match = value.match(/^([0-9,.]+)(K|M|B)?(\+)?$/i); // ... returns parsed components } ``` Parses strings like: - "10K+" → { number: 10, suffix: "K+", isAnimatable: true } - "7" → { number: 7, suffix: "", isAnimatable: true } - "~15M" → { number: 0, suffix: "~15M", isAnimatable: false } ### Dependency Array The `useMemo` dependency is `[stats]` because: - `stats` prop changes rarely (only on data refresh) - If stats change, we need to reparse - No other dependencies affect the parsing ## Isomorphic Guarantee ✅ **Same output**: useMemo caches the result of the same computation ✅ **Same behavior**: Parsed values are identical, just computed less often ✅ **No visual change**: Users see exactly the same animated numbers ## Files Modified - `components/stats-grid.tsx` - Line 3: Added `useMemo` to imports - Lines 118-122: Added memoized parsing - Line 149: Updated map to use pre-computed values ## Verification ```bash npm run lint # 0 errors npm run build # Success, 45 pages generated ``` ## Performance Impact - **Before**: 4 regex operations per render - **After**: 4 regex operations only when `stats` prop changes - **Typical improvement**: Eliminates ~20-50 redundant parses per user session","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:50:48.270056180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:44.158148966Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:44.158148966Z","close_reason":"Implemented useMemo for parsedStats array in stats-grid.tsx. Prevents re-parsing on every render cycle. `tests/e2e/tldr-diagram.spec.ts` ```typescript import { test, expect } from "@playwright/test"; test.describe("TL;DR Interactive Synergy Diagram", () => { test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Navigating to /tldr'); await page.goto("/tldr"); // Scroll diagram into view console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Scrolling diagram into view'); await page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]').scrollIntoViewIfNeeded(); }); test.describe("Basic Rendering", () => { test("should display all core tool nodes", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Verifying all nodes visible'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); await expect(diagram).toBeVisible(); // Check for each core tool node const coreTools = ['NTM', 'SLB', 'Mail', 'BV', 'UBS', 'CM', 'CASS']; for (const tool of coreTools) { console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Checking for ${tool} node`); await expect(diagram.locator(`text=${tool}`)).toBeVisible(); } console.log('[E2E:Diagram] All core tool nodes present'); }); test("should display connection lines between tools", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Verifying connection lines'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const lines = diagram.locator('line, path').filter({ has: page.locator('[stroke]') }); const lineCount = await lines.count(); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Found ${lineCount} connection lines`); expect(lineCount).toBeGreaterThan(5); // Should have multiple connections console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Connection lines verified'); }); test("should display dynamic core tools count", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Checking dynamic count'); const countLabel = page.locator('text=/\\d+ Core Tools/'); await expect(countLabel).toBeVisible(); const text = await countLabel.textContent(); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Center label: "${text}"`); // Verify it's a number followed by "Core Tools" expect(text).toMatch(/^\d+ Core Tools$/); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Dynamic count verified'); }); }); test.describe("Hover Interactions", () => { test("should highlight node on hover", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing node hover'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const ntmNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=NTM') }); // Get initial opacity/style const initialStyles = await ntmNode.evaluate((el) => ({ opacity: getComputedStyle(el).opacity, transform: getComputedStyle(el).transform, })); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Initial NTM styles: ${JSON.stringify(initialStyles)}`); // Hover await ntmNode.hover(); await page.waitForTimeout(100); // Animation time // Check for visual change (scale, opacity, glow, etc.) const hoveredStyles = await ntmNode.evaluate((el) => ({ opacity: getComputedStyle(el).opacity, transform: getComputedStyle(el).transform, })); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Hovered NTM styles: ${JSON.stringify(hoveredStyles)}`); // At least one property should change const hasChange = initialStyles.opacity !== hoveredStyles.opacity || initialStyles.transform !== hoveredStyles.transform; expect(hasChange).toBe(true); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Node hover effect verified'); }); test("should dim non-connected nodes when one is hovered", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing dimming of non-connected nodes'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); // Hover on NTM (which connects to SLB, Mail, CASS) const ntmNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=NTM') }); await ntmNode.hover(); await page.waitForTimeout(150); // UBS should be dimmed (not directly connected to NTM) const ubsNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=UBS') }); const ubsOpacity = await ubsNode.evaluate((el) => getComputedStyle(el).opacity); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] UBS opacity when NTM hovered: ${ubsOpacity}`); expect(parseFloat(ubsOpacity)).toBeLessThan(1); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Non-connected nodes dimmed'); }); test("should highlight connection lines to hovered node", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing connection line highlighting'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const ntmNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=NTM') }); await ntmNode.hover(); await page.waitForTimeout(150); // Check for highlighted lines (increased opacity, different stroke, glow filter) const highlightedLines = diagram.locator('[data-highlighted="true"], .connection-highlighted'); const highlightedCount = await highlightedLines.count(); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Highlighted connection count: ${highlightedCount}`); // NTM has synergies with SLB, Mail, CASS - should have at least 3 highlighted lines expect(highlightedCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Connection highlighting verified'); }); }); test.describe("Click Navigation", () => { test("should scroll to tool card when node is clicked", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing click navigation'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const mailNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=Mail') }); // Get initial scroll position const initialScroll = await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollY); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Initial scroll: ${initialScroll}`); // Click the Mail node await mailNode.click(); // Wait for smooth scroll await page.waitForTimeout(800); // Scroll position should have changed const newScroll = await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollY); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Scroll after click: ${newScroll}`); expect(newScroll).not.toBe(initialScroll); // Mail tool card should be visible in viewport const mailCard = page.locator('[data-testid="tool-card"]').filter({ hasText: 'MCP Agent Mail' }); await expect(mailCard).toBeInViewport(); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Tool card scrolled into view'); }); test("should highlight target card after navigation", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing card highlight after click'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const bvNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=BV') }); await bvNode.click(); await page.waitForTimeout(800); // Card should have highlight state const bvCard = page.locator('[data-testid="tool-card"]').filter({ hasText: 'Beads Viewer' }); // Check for highlight class or ring const hasHighlight = await bvCard.evaluate((el) => { return el.classList.contains('ring-2') || el.classList.contains('highlighted') || el.getAttribute('data-highlighted') === 'true'; }); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Card highlight state: ${hasHighlight}`); expect(hasHighlight).toBe(true); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Card highlighting verified'); }); test("should expand collapsed card after navigation", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing auto-expand on click'); // First, make sure all cards are collapsed const collapseButtons = page.getByRole('button', { name: /collapse all/i }); const collapseCount = await collapseButtons.count(); for (let i = 0; i < collapseCount; i++) { await collapseButtons.nth(i).click(); } const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const cassNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=CASS') }); await cassNode.click(); await page.waitForTimeout(1000); // The CASS card should be expanded const cassCard = page.locator('[data-testid="tool-card"]').filter({ hasText: 'Coding Agent Session Search' }); const expandedContent = cassCard.getByText(/why it's useful/i); await expect(expandedContent).toBeVisible(); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Card auto-expanded after navigation'); }); }); test.describe("Keyboard Navigation", () => { test("should support Tab navigation through nodes", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing Tab navigation'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); await diagram.focus(); // Tab through nodes for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) { await page.keyboard.press('Tab'); const focused = await page.locator(':focus').textContent(); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Tab ${i + 1} focused: ${focused}`); } // A node should be focused const focusedElement = page.locator('svg :focus'); await expect(focusedElement).toBeVisible(); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Tab navigation works'); }); test("should navigate on Enter key when node focused", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing Enter key navigation'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const ntmNode = diagram.locator('[role="button"]').first(); await ntmNode.focus(); await page.keyboard.press('Enter'); await page.waitForTimeout(800); // Should have scrolled to card const ntmCard = page.locator('[data-testid="tool-card"]').filter({ hasText: 'Named Tmux Manager' }); await expect(ntmCard).toBeInViewport(); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Enter key navigation works'); }); }); test.describe("Reduced Motion", () => { test("should respect prefers-reduced-motion", async ({ page }) => { console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Testing reduced motion'); await page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: 'reduce' }); await page.goto('/tldr'); const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy"]'); const ntmNode = diagram.locator('g').filter({ has: page.locator('text=NTM') }); // Hover should still change state but without animation await ntmNode.hover(); // Check that transition duration is 0 or very short const transitionDuration = await ntmNode.evaluate((el) => getComputedStyle(el).transitionDuration ); console.log(`[E2E:Diagram] Transition duration: ${transitionDuration}`); expect(['0s', '0ms', '0.01ms']).toContain(transitionDuration); console.log('[E2E:Diagram] Reduced motion respected'); }); }); }); ``` ## Dependencies - Requires interactive diagram feature (eeo) to be implemented - Nodes need `role="button"` for accessibility - Cards need `data-testid="tool-card"` attributes ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All diagram E2E tests pass - [ ] Hover highlighting tested - [ ] Dimming of non-connected nodes tested - [ ] Click navigation to cards tested - [ ] Card highlighting after navigation tested - [ ] Keyboard accessibility tested - [ ] Reduced motion {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r0i","title":"Epic: TL;DR Page World-Class UI/UX Overhaul","description":"# TL;DR Page World-Class UI/UX Overhaul ## Overview Transform the /tldr page from a functional developer-oriented page to a world-class, Stripe-level polished showcase that makes visitors gasp at its sophistication and attention to detail. ## Background & Context The TL;DR page showcases 13 open-source Flywheel tools. Current implementation is solid but has: - Several bugs affecting data consistency and accessibility - Missing interactive features expected in modern UIs - Mobile experience that's cramped rather than optimized - Lack of micro-interactions and polish that define premium experiences ## Goals 1. Fix all bugs and accessibility issues (Tier 1) 2. Add high-impact UX features: search, interactive diagram, copy buttons (Tier 2) 3. Add premium polish: animations, visual depth, choreography (Tier 3) 4. Future-proof with advanced features: comparison mode, live data (Tier 4) ## Success Criteria - Zero accessibility violations (axe-core clean) - All touch targets ≥44px - Lighthouse performance score ≥90 - Sub-100ms interaction response times - Works flawlessly on mobile and desktop - Visitors comment on the polish and smoothness ## Key Files - app/tldr/page.tsx - Main page component - components/tldr-hero.tsx - Hero section with stats - components/tldr-tool-grid.tsx - Tool card grid with sections - components/tldr-tool-card.tsx - Individual tool cards - components/tldr-synergy-diagram.tsx - SVG synergy visualization - lib/content.ts - Tool data (tldrFlywheelTools, tldrPageData) ## Technical Considerations - Must respect prefers-reduced-motion throughout - Must maintain <100KB JS for initial load - All animations via Framer Motion for consistency - Mobile-first responsive approach - No breaking changes to existing URL structure ## Reference Standards - Stripe.com product pages (micro-interactions, depth) - Linear.app (keyboard navigation, polish) - Vercel.com (dark mode excellence, typography)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:49:09.758995199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:38:03.109094835Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:38:03.109028602Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r1ut","title":"Fix mismatched crossOrigin in Google Analytics preconnect","description":"The preconnect links for GA in layout.tsx use crossOrigin='anonymous', but the script is loaded without CORS. This prevents connection reuse. Fix: Remove crossOrigin attribute.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:08:08.206850799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T22:09:16.570508688Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T22:09:16.570508688Z","close_reason":"Removed crossOrigin attribute","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r37","title":"Pre-flight: Check for existing content to reuse for renamed projects","description":"## What Before adding ntm and slb entries, search the entire codebase for any existing descriptions, documentation, or content we can reuse from their original names (named_tmux_manager, simultaneous_launch_button). ## Why - User explicitly said: "ntm is a renaming of named_tmux_manager so keep that in mind and reuse the sections we already have written" - User explicitly said: "slb is a renaming of simultaneous_launch_button so keep that in mind and reuse the sections we already have written" - We must NOT lose any existing work or descriptions - Reusing existing content ensures consistency and saves effort ## Search Locations 1. `lib/content.ts` - Check projects array for named_tmux_manager or simultaneous_launch_button entries 2. `content/writing/` - Check markdown files for mentions or dedicated articles 3. Any other .md, .tsx, .ts files that might reference these projects 4. Check git history if entries were previously removed ## Implementation ```bash # Search for existing content grep -rn "named_tmux_manager\|simultaneous_launch_button" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.md" . grep -rn "tmux.*manager\|launch.*button" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.md" . # Check git history for removed content git log --all --full-history -p -- '**/content.ts' | grep -A 20 -B 5 "named_tmux\|simultaneous_launch" ``` ## Output Create a document (or note in this bead) with: - Any existing descriptions found - Any existing tags found - Any blog posts or articles referencing these projects - Recommendation on what to reuse vs write fresh ## Dependencies - This task should be completed BEFORE adding ntm (ka2) and slb (ecn) ## Acceptance Criteria - Comprehensive search performed - Any existing content documented for reuse - Clear guidance provided for ntm and slb addition tasks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:33:54.770030767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:23:07.956002088Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:23:07.956002088Z","close_reason":"Completed: Found and updated existing entries for ntm (Named Tmux Manager) and slb (Simultaneous Launch Button) - updated star counts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
